privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: All image resizing happens locally in your browser using HTML5 Canvas. Your images never leave your device.
privacy.p2: No uploads to any server. No storage. No tracking of your image content.
privacy.p3: Your privacy is 100% protected - everything runs client-side on your machine.
privacy.p4: Close the tab anytime. Your original images remain untouched.
privacy.notice: All processing happens in your browser, no uploads.

howTo.title: How To Resize WebP Image Online
howTo.step1Desc: Drop your WebP images into the upload area. Everything is processed locally in your browser.
howTo.step2Desc: Set exact pixel sizes, scale by percentage, or pick a preset. Use constraints to keep images looking natural.
howTo.step3Desc: Download resized WebP files individually or as a ZIP. Adjust quality for the best balance.
howTo.tipText: WebP offers great compression and transparency support. Use the quality slider to balance file size and image quality.

why.title: Why Resize WebP?
why.p1: Resizing WebP images helps you serve correctly sized, next-gen images. Proper dimensions improve load time and Core Web Vitals while keeping quality high.
why.tipText: Use WebP for web delivery to reduce bandwidth while keeping sharp images.

platform.title: Perfect Size for Every Platform
platform.card1Desc: Resize WebP images while preserving alpha transparency and clean edges.
platform.card2Desc: Serving correctly sized images improves performance and helps your pages rank better.
platform.card3Desc: Resize many WebP images at once directly in the browser with no uploads.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.introPrefix: Different output formats produce different file sizes.
qualityGuide.introMiddle1: offers the smallest files, followed by
qualityGuide.introSuffix: Adjust the quality slider to see how it affects file size.
qualityGuide.recommendedDesc: Web images • Social media • Best balance

items.question: Does resizing WebP keep transparency?
items.answer: Yes. WebP supports alpha transparency, and resizing in the browser preserves it as long as your output format remains WebP or PNG.

items.question: Why resize if WebP is already small?
items.answer: Even efficient formats waste bandwidth when dimensions are too large. Resizing to the actual display size improves speed and reduces decode cost.

items.question: What quality should I use for WebP?
items.answer: For most web use, 80–95% quality is a strong balance between file size and visual clarity.

items.question: Is this WebP resizer private?
items.answer: Yes. Everything runs locally in your browser and your images are never uploaded to a server.
